channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Highlanders chairman, vice chairman and treasurer killed in horror road crash @nehandaradio
item.description: Highlanders FC has been plunged into mourning after chairman Kenneth Mhlophe, vice chairman Fiso Siziba and treasurer Nkani Khoza were killed in a horrific road crash near Bulawayo. The post Highlanders chairman, vice chairman and treasurer killed in horror road crash appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zimbabwe joins BRICS New Development Bank
item.description: Xinhua Zimbabwe has officially joined the BRICS New Development Bank (NDB), Finance, Economic Development and Investment Promotion Minister Mthuli Ncube announced on Thursday. Speaking at the Zimbabwe Industrialization Conference and Expo in Harare, Ncube confirmed that Zimbabwe was admitted as a member of the Shanghai-based institution on Wednesday, unlocking crucial credit and funding opportunities for [...] The post Zimbabwe joins BRICS New Development Bank appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Teachers urge govt to reintegrate educators returning from South Africa
item.description: TEACHERS have urged the government to reintegrate Zimbabwean educators returning from South Africa into the country’s education system following their repatriation amid a wave of xenophobic attacks. Over 100,000 Zimbabweans have returned home after fleeing anti-immigrant violence in South Africa, with many abandoning their jobs and businesses. The returnees now face the challenge of rebuilding [...] The post Teachers urge govt to reintegrate educators returning from South Africa appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe fall by 7 wickets to India in T20I match
item.description: Zimbabwe’s senior men’s cricket team began its T20I series against India with a 7-wicket defeat at Harare Sports Club on Thursday. The Chevrons went into the match on the back of Sunday’s defeat to Bangladesh at Queens Sports Club, where they lost the three-match T20I series 2-1. Head coach Justin Sammons named a largely unchanged [...] The post Zimbabwe fall by 7 wickets to India in T20I match appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe capacity utilisation up 61,2% as exports rake in US$584 million
item.description: ZIMBABWE’s manufacturing capacity utilisation has surged to 61, 2% attributed to improved utilisation, which has seen export earnings raking in US$584 million. Addressing stakeholders at the Zimbabwe Industrialisation Conference Expo (ZICE) 2026, Industry and Commerce Minister Mangaliso Ndlovu said various strategies employed since 2019 have spurred industrial capacity. “Manufacturing capacity utilisation has increased significantly from [...] The post Zimbabwe capacity utilisation up 61,2% as exports rake in US$584 million appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mutapa Gold secures US$125m loan facility for Shamva Hill project
item.description: STATE owned miner Mutapa Gold Resources (MGR) has secured a US$125 million syndicated loan from local banks to finance the development of its Shamva Hill Project. The banking syndicate includes: CBZ Bank (US$25 million), Ecobank (US$25 million), CABS (US$20 million), ZB (US$15 million) NMB (US$15 million), FBC Bank (US$10 million), First Capital (US$10 million) and [...] The post Mutapa Gold secures US$125m loan facility for Shamva Hill project appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Can ‘The Polygamist’ open the door for more Zimbabwean adaptations?
item.description: Mail & Guardian Netflix’s global success with Sue Nyathi’s ‘The Polygamist’ has reignited calls to adapt Zimbabwe’s celebrated novels for film and television. But filmmakers say turning literary gems into screen hits requires far more than great stories After Sue Nyathi’s The Polygamist became a Netflix global sensation, reaching number two on Netflix’s global non-English [...] The post Can ‘The Polygamist’ open the door for more Zimbabwean adaptations? appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Chinese firms help Zimbabwe secure $300m for lithium mine
item.description: Mutapa Energy Resources, Zimbabwe’s state-owned lithium miner, has secured $300 million from a group of companies, which includes Chinese entities, to develop its facilities in the southern African nation. “It’s a done deal,” Innocent Rukweza, chief executive officer of Mutapa Energy said in an interview. He declined to identify the companies because one firm was [...] The post Chinese firms help Zimbabwe secure $300m for lithium mine appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: ‘We are coming for every undocumented foreign national, no matter the race,’ Cape Town March and March leader says
item.description: IOL Anti-undocumented migrant groups marched through Cape Town’s CBD on Thursday, declaring that their campaign targets all undocumented foreign nationals regardless of nationality or race, as they handed a memorandum to the Department of Home Affairs demanding stronger immigration enforcement. Members of March and March, the Labour and Civic Organisation (LACO), Operation Dudula and supporters braved [...] The post ‘We are coming for every undocumented foreign national, no matter the race,’ Cape Town March and March leader says appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: President reaffirms importance of strong Govt, Church ties
item.description: Debra Matabvu-Senior Reporter PRESIDENT MNANGAGWA has reaffirmed the importance of a strong partnership between the Government and the Church in promoting peace, national unity and socio-economic development. This followed a... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Government fast-tracks reintegration of youth returnees
item.description: Ray Bande Senior Reporter THE Government is carrying out a registration and training needs assessment for young Zimbabweans returning from South Africa as part of efforts to facilitate their reintegration... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Kasambabezi Border Post to unlock Binga’s economic potential: Ministert Kazembe @bulawayo24
item.description: BINGA - For years, Binga residents watched trucks and traders take long detours to cross into Zambia. That changes this week.The Kasambabezi Border Post, linking Zimbabwe and Zambia through Binga, wil... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: WATCH: Wessly Madhevere on Harare pitch Conditions, top-order collapse & facing India’s bowling @3menonaboat
item.description:
Returning Zimbabwe batter Wessly Madhevere gives his honest post-match reaction following Zimbabwe’s performance in the 1st T20I against India at Harare Sports Club.

The post WATCH: Wessly Madhevere on Harare pitch Conditions, top-order collapse & facing India’s bowling first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: School bus hit with US$30 fine over Route Authority requirement, questions raised @bulawayo24 @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: A traffic enforcement operation by the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) in Mbembesi has sparked questions after a school bus was allegedly issued with a US$30 deposit fine for operating without a Route... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Parliamentary committee recommends Commission of Inquiry to resolve Hwange governance crisis @bulawayo24
item.description: The Parliamentary Portfolio Committee on Local Government, Public Works and National Housing has recommended the establishment of a Commission of Inquiry to resolve long‐standing governance challenges... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News
